name: Alamo Square Park
description: The Painted Ladies panorama, steep hill, Victorian row houses, popular with tourists and dog walkers

name: Alcatraz Island
description: Former federal penitentiary, ferry from Pier 33, bay views, seabirds

name: Aquatic Park
description: Protected cove, cold-water swimmers, bocce ball, Maritime Museum, cable car terminus nearby

name: Baker Beach
description: Clothing-optional beach with Golden Gate Bridge backdrop, Marin Headlands view

name: Bernal Heights Park
description: Summit park with 360° views, dogs off-leash all over the hill, radio tower, beloved by locals

name: Buena Vista Park
description: Forested hilltop park, oldest park in SF, overgrown trails, panoramic views, cruising history

name: California Academy of Sciences
description: Natural history — living roof, aquarium, planetarium, rainforest dome, all under one roof in GG Park

name: City Hall
description: Gilded dome (taller than DC's Capitol), Harvey Milk was shot here, civic events

name: Coit Tower
description: WPA murals inside, Telegraph Hill views, feral parrots, steps from North Beach

name: Crissy Field
description: Restored tidal marsh and beach along the bay, Golden Gate views, cyclists, dog walkers, kite fliers

name: Dolores Park
description: Mission's social hub — picnics, views of downtown, Castro on one side, J-Church streetcar

name: Duboce Park
description: Popular dog park, N-Judah streetcar passes alongside, Duboce Triangle neighborhood

name: Embarcadero Waterfront
description: Ferry Building to Bay Bridge — promenade, farmers market, bay views, tourist ferries

name: Ferry Building
description: 1898 Beaux-Arts terminal, Saturday farmers market, artisan food vendors, bay views

name: Fillmore Auditorium
description: Legendary music venue, posters are part of the mythology, shaped SF rock history

name: Fort Mason
description: WWII port turned cultural center — SFMOMA rental, farmer markets, theater, Bay views

name: Glen Canyon Park
description: Wild canyon in the middle of the city, creek, native plants, neighborhood dogs

name: Golden Gate Park
description: 1,017-acre urban park — de Young museum, Japanese Tea Garden, Conservatory of Flowers, bison paddock, dutch windmills, Polo Field

name: Haight & Ashbury Street
description: The intersection — street sign tourists, head shops, Summer of Love memorial ground zero

name: Lafayette Park
description: Sunken city block park, Pacific Heights living rooms overflow here, dogs, tennis courts

name: Lands End
description: Rugged coastal bluffs, labyrinth, shipwreck views, Sutro Baths ruins, GGNRA trail

name: McLaren Park
description: Second-largest park in SF, overlooked by tourists, community gathering, hiking, disc golf

name: Ocean Beach
description: Wild Pacific shore — strong undertow, surfers in wetsuits, bonfires at night, foggy and windswept

name: Palace of Fine Arts
description: Roman rotunda and colonnade reflected in a lagoon, swans, wedding photos, the Marina's jewel

name: Precita Eyes Murals
description: Mission mural tradition radiating from Precita Park, community art tours

name: SFMOMA
description: Modern art anchor of SoMa, expanded Snøhetta wing, free to walk through ground floor

name: Sutro Baths
description: 1896 glass-enclosed baths ruins, now a tidal pool at low tide, atmospheric

name: Tartine Manufactory
description: The bread line that defines the Mission's food culture — sourdough, pastries, coffee

name: Twin Peaks
description: Two 922-foot summits, panoramic 360° city view, tourist overlook and local hiking

name: de Young Museum
description: Fine arts, American art, textiles, tower with free views over GG Park, Golden Gate Bridge
